L1.area: Input reading and byte stream consumption logic

L2.description: An integer underflow vulnerability exists in the error handling of a byte-reading operation. When a slice retrieval fails, the internal index is still advanced, leaving the reader in an inconsistent state. Subsequent operations that rely on the index being within bounds may underflow, leading to a panic and potential denial of service.

L3.area: Byte stream reader and input slicing routines
L3.description: An integer underflow vulnerability exists in the error handling of a byte-skipping and input-slicing operation. When the requested slice exceeds available input, the error is not returned early, causing the internal read position to advance past the valid range. Subsequent length calculations underflow, leading to a panic and potential denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: A mistake in error handling in the skip_and_get_input method causes the Reader's index to advance even when a slice retrieval fails, leading to an integer underflow and panic in subsequent operations.
